title: The Holy Quran
description: The central religious text of Islam, known for its spiritual guidance and classical Arabic prose.

title: The Secret of Secrets
author: Dan Brown
description: A thrilling mystery involving ancient symbols, secret societies, and scientific discovery.

title: Much Too Vulgar
author: Viggy Parr Hampton
description: A brash, unapologetic horror novel featuring anatomical themes and a dark, satirical edge.

title: Samyabadi
author: Kazi Nazrul Islam
description: A revolutionary collection of poems advocating for human equality and social justice.

title: Choritrohin
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: A landmark Bengali novel exploring morality and complex human relationships.

title: Himu Remande
author: Humayun Ahmed
description: The anti-logic hero Himu finds himself in a police remand, handling it with his unique philosophy.

title: Pather Panchali
author: Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay
description: A coming-of-age story of Apu and Durga in a small Bengali village, reflecting rural life.

title: French Revolution: A History From Beginning to End
author: Hourly History
description: A detailed historical overview of the political upheaval that reshaped modern France.

title: Bhagavad Gita As It Is
author: A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
description: A profound philosophical dialogue between Prince Arjuna and Lord Krishna on the battlefield.

title: The Big Book of Dad Jokes
author: Joe Kerz
description: 800 groan-inducing puns and riddles to keep the family laughing (or sighing).

title: The Dying Detective
author: Leif GW Persson
description: A gripping mystery novel centered around a retired detective solving a cold case from his deathbed.

title: Die Bibel
author: Multiple Authors
description: The German edition of the Holy Bible, the foundational sacred text of Christianity.

title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The third installment in the world-renowned series, following Harry as he discovers the truth about Sirius Black.

title: Sophie's World
author: Jostein Gaarder
description: A novel that doubles as a comprehensive introduction to the history of Western philosophy.

title: Gitanjali
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A Nobel Prize-winning collection of poems translated by William Radice, exploring themes of devotion and nature.
